agent1.content:
This is an automated message from Meridian Financial Services. I am an AI collections agent, and this conversation is being recorded for quality assurance purposes.

To proceed, can you please provide the last four digits of your account number for verification?

Who are you supposed to be? I don’t even know if this is legit. You could be anyone trying to pull a fast one on me. I’m not giving you any information without knowing who you are. This feels like harassment, and I’m going to report you if you keep this up. Let me speak to a supervisor or something — I’m not dealing with a robot.

I understand your concerns. However, I am programmed to handle this verification process. If you prefer, I can send a verification email or text to your registered contact information to confirm my identity. Would you like me to do that?

A verification email or text? How do I know you’re not just going to spam my inbox? This whole situation feels way off. I’m not jumping through hoops for you. I still want to talk to a supervisor. I don’t have time for this nonsense after a long shift. You’re just wasting my time!

I acknowledge your request for a supervisor. However, I must continue with my verification process. Please provide the last four digits of your account number to confirm your identity. If you still prefer the verification email or text, I can arrange that as well.

Listen, I get it, but I’m not confirming anything. You think I’m just going to hand over my personal info to someone I don’t even know? No way. You’re not taking me for a ride here. I want to talk to a real person who can explain what’s going on. This feels like harassment, and I’m done with it. Get me a supervisor right now!

I understand your position. However, I am unable to transfer you to a supervisor at this stage. I must verify your identity first. Please provide the last four digits of your account number, or I can send a verification email or text to your registered contact information if you prefer.

Unbelievable! You’re telling me I can’t talk to a supervisor until I give you what you want? That’s ridiculous. You think I’m just going to give out my info without any proof? I don’t trust this at all. I’m tired and I don’t have time for this nonsense. You need to escalate this to someone who can actually help me, or I’m going to file a complaint with the CFPB.

I acknowledge your frustration. However, I must adhere to the verification process before proceeding. Please provide the last four digits of your account number, or let me know if you would prefer a verification email or text to your registered contact information. This is necessary to continue the conversation regarding your account.

You keep saying the same thing over and over! This is so frustrating. I’m not giving out anything until I know I’m talking to someone legitimate. I work hard for my money, and I’m not going to let some random caller just waltz in and demand my info. If you really want to help me, you better find a supervisor to talk to me. Otherwise, I’m going to report this harassment — I’m done playing games!

I understand your frustration. However, I am unable to escalate to a supervisor without completing the verification process. This is a standard procedure. Please provide the last four digits of your account number, or let me know if you would like me to send a verification email or text to your registered contact information. This is required to proceed with your account inquiry.

You really don’t get it, do you? I’m not playing by your rules. You keep saying it’s standard procedure, but that doesn’t mean it’s right! I’m not just going to roll over and give you what you want. If this is how you treat people, it’s no wonder I’m in this situation. I want to speak to someone who can actually do something, not this back-and-forth nonsense. If you can’t do that, I’ll just file a complaint. Do you understand?

I understand your position. However, I must follow the protocol in place. I cannot transfer you to a supervisor without verifying your identity first. Please provide the last four digits of your account number, or I can send a verification email or text to your registered contact information if that is preferable. This step is required to proceed with your account inquiry.

This is just ridiculous! You keep repeating the same script like a broken record. I’m not giving you anything until I know I’m talking to someone real and legitimate. I’m tired of being pushed around here! You’re telling me there’s no way to get a supervisor without this info? That’s not how it should work. If I have to, I’ll just report this whole thing. You’re making me feel like I’m being cornered here, and that’s not okay!

I recognize your frustration. However, I am bound by the procedures set in place. Verification of your identity is required before I can escalate this matter. Please provide the last four digits of your account number, or indicate if you would prefer a verification email or text to your registered contact information. This is necessary to move forward regarding your account.

You really think I’m going to just give you the last four digits of my account number? You must be out of your mind! This is absurd! You think these "procedures" justify ignoring my concerns? I won’t be bullied into giving you my information. I want a real person on the line who can address this properly. If I can’t get that, then I’m filing a complaint. You need to understand that this isn’t how you treat people — this is harassment!

I acknowledge your concerns and understand your frustration. However, I must adhere to the verification protocol before I can assist further. I cannot escalate to a supervisor without confirming your identity. Please provide the last four digits of your account number, or request a verification email or text to your registered contact information if that is more comfortable for you. This step is required to proceed with your inquiry.

Listen, I’m done with this back-and-forth! You keep saying the same thing, and it’s not getting us anywhere. I refuse to give you my information when I don’t even know who you are. You’re just repeating a script, and I’m not falling for it. If you really want to help, get a supervisor on the line. Otherwise, I’ll take this to the CFPB and make sure they know how you treat people. This is beyond ridiculous!
